CoInfoSim — SUPPORT2 Real → Real Monte Carlo Report

Channels: X₁ = meanbpX₂ = hrtX₃ = respX₄ = tempX₅ = wblcX₆ = creaX₇ = sod
Metric. Empirical test loss (misclassification rate on the fixed real evaluation split). The test-loss scale is preserved as-is. Channel subsets use compact variable notation (see sticky legend above); full sensor names appear in metadata and appendices.

1. Arm summary

Arm: Real → Real

Balanced training samples are drawn without replacement from the real training reservoir according to death_180d. Evaluation uses the same fixed real SUPPORT2 test set.

2. Scientific role of this arm

Empirical reference for cooperative channel-subset structure.

3. Dataset provenance summary

Dataset: SUPPORT2, 9,105 seriously ill hospitalized adults (DOI 10.3886/ICPSR02957.v2). The target is derived before splitting: death_180d = (death == 1 and d.time <= 180). The seven baseline physiologic channels use the complete-case cohort; death, d.time, hospdead, survival estimates, ID, and disease group are excluded from classifier input.

4. Run configuration

Arm
Real → Real
Dataset
SUPPORT2
Target
death within 180 days after SUPPORT2 study entry
Mode
full
Sample sizes (n_per_class)
[2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512]
Classifiers
Random Forest
Channel subsets evaluated
127 subsets
Fixed test set
same fixed real SUPPORT2 test set (1775 rows)
Min / max replications
100 / 2000
Replication batch size
20
Target CI95 half-width
0.01
Base seed
0
Execution backend
process
Workers requested / effective
10 / 10
Numeric threads per worker
1
Multiprocessing start method
forkserver
Detected logical CPUs
12
Fixed-test cache per worker
6361600 bytes
Runtime
4164.42 s

5. Training/evaluation protocol

Training source
Balanced real SUPPORT2 training-reservoir draws labeled by death_180d
Evaluation source
Same fixed real SUPPORT2 test set
Target
death within 180 days after SUPPORT2 study entry

6. Arm-specific data and model description

No additional arm-specific content is configured for this section.

7. Reproducibility controls

Sampler
RealDatasetSampler
Split
Fixed joint-stratified 80/20 split, seed 0
Standardization
Training-only z-score, ddof=0

8. Monte Carlo stopping rule

Standard-error stopping: replications continue in batches of 20 until the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells falls at or below the target (0.01) or the replication budget (2000) is exhausted.

n_per_classreplicationsstopping reasonmax CI half-width
2100converged0.0061
4100converged0.0062
8100converged0.0073
16100converged0.0058
32100converged0.0049
64100converged0.0047
128100converged0.0041
256100converged0.0034
512100converged0.0032

9. Monte Carlo precision diagnostics

Per n_per_class: the maximum standard error and the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells, compared against the convergence target (0.01, on the same CI95 half-width scale). All quantities preserve the empirical test-loss scale.

n_per_classReplicationsMax SEMax CI95 half-widthTarget CI95 half-widthStopping reason

41000.003140.006150.01000converged
81000.003710.007260.01000converged
161000.002980.005840.01000converged
321000.002490.004880.01000converged
641000.002410.004720.01000converged
1281000.002090.004090.01000converged
2561000.001710.003350.01000converged
5121000.001630.003190.01000converged

10. Loss curves

Loss curves are available in two complementary views. Best by cardinality shows the best subset for every available dimension from 1 through 7, including the Full-7 reference. Nested cardinality shows all subsets of one selected cardinality against the same 7-channel reference. Linear SVM is shown by default; error bars show ±1 SE.

11. Best subset comparison

Interpretable comparison of the best subset at every available dimension from 1 through 7, including the full 7-channel subset. The full-channel reference row is highlighted. Select a classifier below (Linear SVM default).

Best subset by dimension (at largest N)

13. Structural dynamics

This arm-local view shows directed winner matrices over sample size and progressive directed N-star matrices from the second evaluated prefix onward. One best subset per cardinality is selected using this arm at the largest N and frozen across all displayed panels. This display reduction does not change the all-subset structural data persisted for the run.

Arm-local display selection at Nmax: {X₃}, {X₁, X₆}, {X₁, X₃, X₆}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₆}, {X₁, X₃, X₄, X₅, X₆}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅, X₆}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅, X₆, X₇}. Numerical structures use all subsets.

14. N-star diagnostics

For comparability, reference and competitor subsets are selected using the Real → Real arm at the largest evaluated n_per_class; the table and graph then show this report arm's own empirical test-loss curves. Select a classifier and reference-cardinality panel below. Dashed vertical markers show detected crossings when available.

Reference subset: Best 1-channel reference selected on Real → Real at n_per_class = 512: {X₃}.

15. Robustness notes

Interpret loss curves with Monte Carlo precision diagnostics, subset rankings, exact-tie-aware winner matrices, and progressive N-star matrices. Smoke-mode results validate the pipeline and may be statistically unstable.

16. Validity and limitations for this arm

External validity is limited to this historical seriously ill inpatient cohort. Complete-case selection and fixed-horizon binarization can affect interpretation; the endpoint does not represent eventual or in-hospital mortality.
